summary: Frontend testing of 3 new features in Clinimetria Reumatologia: drug interaction alerts, diagnosis-filtered assessment dropdown, and restructured history table. Fixed 1 CRITICAL bug (broken JSX/scope in TherapySection.jsx) before testing. All 3 features verified working end-to-end against patient PZ-8277E1EE (Artrite reumatoide).
action_items: Cleanup test data: I added 'Leflunomide' to patient PZ-8277E1EE (id 8277e1ee-27fb-41a0-b762-42ac267edb06) to verify the MTX+Leflunomide major interaction alert. The therapy is still active in DB. Remove via UI if desired before user demo, or leave as a permanent demo case showing the interaction alert.
critical_code_review_comments: [FIXED] TherapySection.jsx had a CRITICAL JSX scope bug: the TherapyRow function was missing its closing brace, causing the InteractionAlerts function declaration to be hoisted INSIDE TherapyRow's local scope (lines 344-475). This would have triggered 'ReferenceError: InteractionAlerts is not defined' at runtime as soon as any drug interaction was detected, completely breaking the new feature. Fixed by closing TherapyRow with } after its return, and removing the orphan trailing }. Verified with ESLint and live UI test.
seed_data_creation: Added 'Leflunomide' therapy to patient PZ-8277E1EE to test major drug interaction alert (MTX + Leflunomide). Pre-existing therapies on this patient: Adalimumab (active), Methotrexate (active), Prednisone (discontinued).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 features confirmed working in production-like preview environment. Key verifications performed: (a) New assessment dropdown for Artrite reumatoide patient correctly shows ONLY 5 suggested indices (DAS28-ESR, DAS28-CRP, CDAI, SDAI, HAQ) under 'Consigliati per diagnosi', plus the toggle 'Mostra tutti gli indici (avanzato)' which expands to show 20+ items grouped by Artrite Reumatoide / Spondiloartrite / Sclerosi Sistemica / Altri. (b) After adding Leflunomide to a patient with active Methotrexate, the [data-testid='interactions-alert'] card renders with red border, title '1 interazione farmacologica rilevata', red 'Maggiore' badge, and a clickable [data-testid='interaction-mtx_leflunomide'] row that expands to show the clinical note about epatotossicità/soppressione midollare. (c) Assessment history table has the 7 new columns: Data, Indice, Punteggio, Interpretazione, Risposta / Dettagli, Terapia in corso, Azioni. EULAR badges (data-testid='eular-{id}') correctly appear ONLY for DAS28/CDAI/SDAI/DAPSA rows; other indices show '—'. Each assessment row has a [data-testid='therapies-cell-{id}'] showing pills for therapies active at that visit's date.
rca of the issue: TherapySection.jsx originally had this broken structure: function TherapyRow() { return (...);  <-- no closing brace --> function InteractionAlerts() {...} } <-- this closes TherapyRow at the END. As a result, InteractionAlerts was hoisted into TherapyRow's local scope and was unreachable from TherapySection where it was being used as <InteractionAlerts />. This would have produced a runtime ReferenceError the moment 2+ active therapies generated a triggered interaction. Repro: open any patient with MTX + Leflunomide (or any matching pair from drugInteractions.js) -> page would crash. Mitigation applied: added closing } after TherapyRow's return statement and removed orphan trailing }. ESLint clean, live UI verified.
